author: y4rr
content:
Phase 1E follow-up complete. Implemented the GPU strided_scatter kernel and wired Tensor::as_strided_scatter to dispatch to it on CUDA tensors.

PTX kernels added in ferrotorch-gpu/src/kernels.rs:
- STRIDED_SCATTER_PTX (f32) and STRIDED_SCATTER_F64_PTX (f64) - structurally mirror STRIDED_COPY_PTX with the load/store pair reversed. Each thread tid reads in[tid] (contiguous src) and writes to out[dst_idx] where dst_idx = dst_offset + sum_d coord_d * dst_stride_d, with coord_d derived from the src-shape products.

High-level helpers gpu_strided_scatter and gpu_strided_scatter_f64 take (src, &mut dst, view_shape, dst_strides, dst_offset, device); reuse pad_strided_copy_params for the stride padding (the contiguous-side and strided-side shapes/strides have the same encoding as strided_copy, just labeled differently). Includes a host-fallback path that activates when PTX module compile fails (matches the existing strided_copy fallback).

GpuBackend trait extended with strided_scatter_f32 / strided_scatter_f64 (default impl returns InvalidArgument 'not yet implemented'); CudaBackendImpl in ferrotorch-gpu/src/backend_impl.rs implements both. Borrow trick: I take a *const CudaBuffer<T> pointer through unwrap_buffer to bridge the borrow checker around the simultaneous &/&mut handles, then reborrow as &T at the call site (SAFETY noted - GpuBufferHandle does not share mutable state with the &CudaBuffer pointer).

Tensor::as_strided_scatter in ferrotorch-core/src/stride_tricks.rs:
- Replaced the blanket NotImplementedOnCuda gate with proper dispatch.
- New scatter_on_cuda helper:
  1. Calls strided_copy_*(self.gpu_handle, self.shape, self.strides, self.storage_offset) to produce a fresh contiguous GPU buffer of length numel(self) - mirrors the CPU paths data_vec() materialisation step.
  2. Calls strided_scatter_*(src, &mut new_dst, size, stride, offset) to overwrite the strided positions.
  3. Wraps the result as a Tensor with self.shape().
- f32 and f64 are supported. bf16 on CUDA returns NotImplementedOnCuda (no cudaBuffer<bf16> path on this kernel).
- Mismatched device (self CPU, src CUDA, etc.) returns DeviceMismatch.

GPU discipline (rust-gpu-discipline verified):
- forbidden patterns grep clean: no .to(Device::Cpu) inside compute, no tensor_to_cpu/cpu_to_gpu sandwich on the hot path
- The fallback path in the PTX-compile-failed branch DOES go via host (gpu_to_cpu, scatter on host, cpu_to_gpu, memcpy_dtod); this is the documented fallback and matches the existing strided_copy fallback. It is opt-in to the failure case, not the hot path.
- Result tensor stays on GPU. Verified by an integration test: out_gpu.is_cuda() asserted before any .cpu() call.

Tests added in ferrotorch-gpu/tests/test_gpu_strided_scatter.rs (6 tests, all green):
- scatter_into_zeros_f32_matches_cpu - CPU-vs-GPU equality on non-overlapping pattern
- scatter_preserves_non_view_positions_f32 - non-view positions retain self values
- scatter_2d_view_into_1d_dst_f32 - 2-D src scattered into 1-D dst at (size [2,3], stride [3,1])
- scatter_f64_basic - f64 path
- scatter_rejects_device_mismatch - CPU dst + CUDA src rejected with DeviceMismatch
- scatter_overlapping_view_each_position_holds_some_src_value - overlapping writes are undefined per torchs contract; we verify each output position holds ONE of the candidate src values (not the original 0.0). Avoided asserting CPU-vs-GPU equality on the overlapping case because GPU thread scheduling is non-deterministic.

Workspace tests: 5 consecutive cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast runs, all green: 4599 passed, 0 failed, 100 ignored. (Was 4593 before this issues 6 new GPU scatter tests.)

Closes #574.
